steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests punctuation between a subject and its verb. The subject is "Calida Garcia Rawles" and the main verb is "was" — nothing should separate them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Rawles—: an em dash here incorrectly interrupts the subject from its verb; no punctuation belongs between a subject and an immediately following verb. ✗
  • Choice B — Rawles:: a colon incorrectly separates the subject from its verb; colons introduce lists or explanations, not predicates. ✗
  • Choice C — Rawles,: a comma incorrectly separates the subject from its verb; commas may set off nonessential phrases but cannot stand alone between a bare subject and verb. ✗
  • Choice D — Rawles: no punctuation between subject and verb is correct — the sentence flows directly from subject to predicate as Standard English requires. ✓
That is the correct answer.
